subject: SOCDS 2025/26 Update Week #6 - PreK Orange
text:
Email Ms. Annemarie
Reminders:
SOCDS is closed on Monday, October 13th.  Confires will be on campus to test the fire alarm system.
SOCDS PEP Mend Food Drive - through next week.  This year, MEND has asked especially for
shelf-stable milk
and
cooking oil
. As always,
non-perishable, non-expired food items
are welcome. In addition,
unopened personal care items
such as toothbrushes, 
toothpaste, and soap are much needed.
SOCDS Kindergarten and PreK Classes will walk to the Fire Department on the morning of Friday, October 17th.  Please return the permission slip that we sent home today, or use the electronic version below.   Please wear your SOCDS School Shirt or a blue t-shirt.
Parent Teacher Conferences - Week of October 27th - sign-ups below
Gardening with Ms. Linda
The leaves on our sugar maple trees are starting to change color and drop to the ground. We learned that sugar maples belong to a 
group of trees that drop their leaves before they go dormant in winter. They are classified as deciduous trees in contrast to our eastern hemlock tree (next to the front entrance of our school) which is evergreen and is classified as a conifer. We started our observations by looking closely at the shape of the sugar maple leaves which are almost as tall as they are wide. They typically have 5 lobes with moderately deep U-shaped notches (sinuses) between lobes. We counted at least 10 sugar maple trees growing at the school so it was easy to find leaves to examine. We compared them to the needle-shaped leaves of the eastern hemlock which have evolved to withstand harsh winters allowing them to stay green all year and retain water and nutrients.
We also 
did some work to clear out our garden beds to prepare for winter. We pulled out a tomato plant and two pepper plants because they won’t have a chance to bear fruit before the first frost date. Both plants are sun-loving, hot weather crops. We were not surprised to see that their fibrous roots were 1.5-2 feet deep and wide.
Parent Teacher Conferences
Zoom
Our teachers are preparing to host
Parent-Teacher Conferences.
These are great opportunities for us to share our observations with you.
Please click on your child's class link below to sign up for a conference time.
If the available dates or times do not work for you, please let me know, and we will arrange an alternative time that fits your schedule.
Two days before your scheduled conference, you will receive a Zoom link from Signup Genius to access your 
meeting.
Weather has Turned!
Please dress your child appropriately to be outside for 30 minutes at a time.  Good foot wear and a warm hat are useful as many children find heavier jackets constraining.
If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child in appropriate gear for the day.  There is no bad weather just poor clothing.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink water 
throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
You are receiving this email because your child attends SOCDS.  If you no longer wish to receive these updates, please unsubscribe below.
